clusters.title: Obama Presidential Center Opens with Emotional Speeches

The center aims to serve as a hub for community engagement and leadership development.

clusters.title: Trump and Meloni Clash Over Photo Claim at G7 Summit
clusters.summary: During the G7 summit, former President Donald Trump claimed that Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni had 'begged' him for a photo. Meloni responded by stating that Trump's assertion was 'totally invented,' expressing her astonishment at the fabricated narrative. The exchange highlights tensions between the two leaders and raises questions about their diplomatic relationship.

items.title: Trump-Meloni spat grows over claim Italian PM 'begged' for photo: 'astonished'
items.description: Giorgia Meloni fires back at Trump after he claimed she "begged" for a photo, calling his statements "completely made up" following the G7 summit.

items.title: Trump ‘totally fabricated’ claim she begged him for a photo, Italy’s Meloni says
items.description: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back.

items.title: Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo
items.description:
US president provokes outrage as PM says he ‘totally invented’ story in interview with Italian media

Italy’s prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, has said Donald Trump “totally invented” a story about her after the US president claimed she begged him to take a photo with her during the G7 summit.

The two former allies had appeared to be getting their relationship back on track by having several one-to-ones on the sidelines at the gathering in Évian after falling out in April over the US-Israeli war in Iran.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Hegseth Criticizes NATO Allies, Reviews US Forces in Europe
clusters.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced a review of American military forces stationed in Europe, citing concerns over NATO allies' contributions to collective security. During his remarks, he criticized these allies for not taking sufficient responsibility for their own defense, calling their actions 'shameful.' This review is part of a broader discussion on NATO reform and the future of U.S. military presence in the region.

clusters.summary: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a seat in Parliament during a special election, positioning himself as a potential challenger to Prime Minister Keir Starmer. This development could impact the leadership dynamics as Burnham seeks to leverage his newfound influence.

clusters.title: Obama: US Situation Worse After 15 Weeks of Iran War
clusters.summary: Former President Barack Obama stated that the United States may be in a worse position now than before the war with Iran, which began in February. He highlighted the significant financial costs and the strain on resources resulting from the conflict. Despite his concerns, Obama expressed hope for the sustainability of a recent ceasefire agreement with Iran.

items.title: Barack Obama says US is ‘worse off’ than before war with Iran
items.description:
Former president nevertheless said in an interview that he was happy to see a ceasefire with Iran and hopes it holds

  • Analysis: Iran peace deal makes clear how far US has been forced to retreat since 2025

Barack Obama has said that after 15 weeks of war with Iran, the US is now “worse off” than before the conflict started in February.

“We’ve now fought a war, spent billions and billions of dollars, you know, put enormous strain on our military. A lot of people have died. And it feels like we’re back where we were before we started the war, except maybe a little bit worse off,” the former US president told NBC News in an interview that aired on Friday.

Continue reading...

items.title: Obama says U.S. may be ‘worse off’ now than before Iran war
items.description: Former President Barack Obama said it seems like the United States has either returned to the status quo or is “worse off” now than before President Donald Trump launched the war on Iran in February.“We’ve now fought a war, spent billions and billions of dollars, you know, put enormous strain on our military.

clusters.summary: The remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur are impacting the Gulf states with heavy rainfall, leading to flash flood warnings and tornado alerts.
